chunks.reason: Default-deny auth middleware, API-vs-browser behavior, CSRF configuration, and cookie hardening.
chunks.excerpt: Auth middleware returns 401/403 JSON for API requests and redirects only for browser routes; CSRF enabled by default; vector and automation blueprints are currently CSRF-exempt.

chunks.reason: Property model already contains rich structured fields (floor/elevator/condition/features) required for smart-context benefits.
chunks.excerpt: Property includes floor_number, has_elevator, property_condition, property_features, custom_fields, listing_type, rahn/ejare and metadata fields useful for contextual translation.

chunks.reason: Existing match/favorite/embedding/job models relevant to scoring and pipeline orchestration.
chunks.excerpt: PropertyMatch, PropertyFavorite, PropertyEmbedding, MatchingJobRun, and RematchQueue already exist and can be extended for customer/property priority scoring.

chunks.reason: Provider already supports retries, timeout controls, strict JSON extraction, and image parsing entrypoint.
chunks.excerpt: GeminiProvider has request timeout and retry controls, _extract_json_object parsing, extract_property_from_image, and generate_recommendation_reasoning methods.

chunks.reason: Current recommendation/extraction service where smart-context and copilot logic can be added with deterministic fallback.
chunks.excerpt: GeminiService already tracks recommendation meta fallback, has extract_property_from_text/customer, and has deterministic fallback recommendation generation.

chunks.reason: Current semantic-hybrid scoring with deterministic fallback metadata and tie-break sorting.
chunks.excerpt: search_properties_with_meta returns mode/is_fallback/reason and computes hybrid_score from semantic + budget + requirements with deterministic sort order.

chunks.reason: Top-level hybrid search orchestration and keyword-only degradation policy.
chunks.excerpt: SearchService applies hybrid weights (semantic 0.70, keyword 0.30) and degrades to keyword-only with explicit fallback metadata.

chunks.reason: Background match scoring, notification generation, and matching cycle lifecycle.
chunks.excerpt: BackgroundMatcher already computes basic fallback match score, saves matches, creates notifications, and logs cycle runs.

chunks.reason: Existing Celery beat schedule and task registration pattern for nightly/weekly scoring jobs.
chunks.excerpt: Celery beat already schedules matching/cleanup/digest jobs; new scoring and training jobs should plug into this schedule model.

chunks.reason: Task wrappers where new scoring and trend jobs can be added safely outside request cycle.
chunks.excerpt: Current Celery tasks delegate to scheduler jobs and sync embeddings; this is the place to add scoring_engine and trend tasks.

chunks.reason: Recommendations, AI parse APIs, export, and viewing schedule routes where matchmaker/copilot endpoints will integrate.
chunks.excerpt: Recommendations already expose fallback metadata and JSON/HTML split behavior; API parse endpoints and schedule-viewing flows exist.

chunks.reason: Property detail/edit/view routes with robust serialization surface for smart-context rendering.
chunks.excerpt: Property detail response already provides rich derived fields and supports JSON fallback under test/degraded conditions.

chunks.reason: Frontend initialization, API request wrapper, CSRF/XHR behavior, and modal lifecycle integration points.
chunks.excerpt: Main JS has central API.request with CSRF and deterministic JSON parsing; good insertion points for badges and copilot trigger UX.

chunks.reason: Modal utilities and request wrapper enforcing CSRF headers and error contracts.
chunks.excerpt: CRUDUtils already handles modal lifecycle, CSRF header propagation in makeRequest, and resilient parsing/feedback for AJAX failures.

chunks.reason: Current recommendation card UI where score badges and copilot CTA can be added.
chunks.excerpt: Template already renders match score and actions; can be extended to Hot Lead / Rare Find badges and Generate Copilot Pitch button.

hard_constraints: No blanket CSRF exemptions on new core endpoints
Browser route auth redirects can remain 302
Gemini/vector failures must degrade deterministically (no 500 for handled paths)
Prefer async/background processing for heavy AI and batch scoring
Keep existing public contracts backward compatible unless explicitly versioned
